close_reason: Doc language is accurate: systemd and process managers do rotate env vars
description: secretx-env/src/lib.rs:21 'refresh re-reads the variable; useful when a process manager rotates it' — the audience for this claim is unclear. A process manager (systemd, supervisord, k8s) cannot rotate an env var of an already-running process; the env block is set at exec time and is not modifiable from outside. Refresh is only useful if THIS process or a sibling thread mutated the variable via std::env::set_var (which is itself unsafe and racy — see crate-secretsx-3btv.2). The doc as written is misleading. Fix: replace with an honest description, e.g. 'refresh re-reads the variable; mainly useful in tests, since environment variables of a running process are normally immutable from outside.'
title: Doc claim 'process manager rotates it' is unclear
